title: fed / fomc / rate cut | Fomc Statement | Transcript of Chair Powell's Press Conference -- September 1...
summary: The Federal Reserve cut the federal funds rate by ¼ percentage point to 4-4¼ percent at the September 17, 2025 FOMC meeting, citing a shift in the balance of risks toward downside employment and upside inflation. Chair Powell acknowledged that tariffs are beginning to push up goods prices while labor market conditions have softened, with payroll gains slowing to just 29,000 per month. The median SEP projection shows the fed funds rate ending 2025 at 3.6 percent, suggesting two additional quarter-point cuts are anticipated.
